data.title: Samsara soars after 'solid' Q2, while analysts remain positive; gets upgrade at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Shares of Samsara (NYSE:IOT) surged about 13% premarket on Friday after fiscal second quarter results and guidance exceeded expectations, which saw largely positive reactions from analysts.

Craig-Hallum upgraded Samsara's stock rating to Buy from Hold and raised the price target on the shares to $48 from $42.

Analysts led by Anthony Stoss said they upgraded the shares as the tariff pause is now behind them and the International business is now growing faster than the U.S. (15% of new wins during quarter). 

The analysts continue to believe Samsara's management is navigating the business well. The company is using Al to enhance the data read-outs for its customers, providing more value. Samsara added the third highest new customer additions in the quarter in its history and signed seven new $1M-plus Annual Recurring Revenue, or ARR, deals in the quarter.

"Further, we highlight IOT has over 350 partner integrations, which we see as a significant growth driver for new customer adds, and they are continuously expanding with the recent addition of dozens more (including Element, Rivian, HappyRobot, and Marsh)," said Stoss and his team.

The analysts noted that the company's management is no longer seeing project delays as they did in April, but they note variability may arise in quarterly results as larger ARR customer additions take longer to close.

Evercore kept its In Line rating with a $45 price target on Samsara's stock.

Analysts led by Kirk Materne said Samsara delivered a stellar quarter, outpacing Evercore/Street estimates across the board, with total revenue of $392M (+31% year-over-year in constant currency) coming in nicely above guidance.

"It was a very solid quarter and while 2H guidance was only modestly bumped up (beyond the F2Q beat), we believe both 2H revenue and NNARR [Net New Annual Recurring Revenue] have room to move higher given the company’s normal level of conservatism around the guide," the analysts added.

Materne and his team believe that seeing revenue growth normalize in the mid-high 20 percentages in the fiscal second half will be key to seeing a more decided turn in the shares over the next three to six months.

Wells Fargo maintained its Overweight rating and $50 price target on the shares.

"IOT's FQ2 helped refuel the bull case w/ NNARR reaccel [reacceleration] to +19%, record $1M+ customer adds & improving profitability," said analysts led by Michael Turrin.

data.title: JPMorgan added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
JPMorgan was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye. 

"We see a balanced, multi-engine model (CCB + CIB + AWM), a fortress balance sheet, and accelerating capital return positioning the stock for meaningful upside," Hedgeye analyst Josh Steiner wrote in a note on Friday.

Steiner added that he sees "macro tailwinds amplifying these strengths through 1H26, while NII resilience (FY25 guide raised to $95.5B), diversified fee growth, and idiosyncratic catalysts create clear upside."

data.title: U.S. payrolls revisions show June weaker, July modestly higher
data.description:
Labor data revisions painted a slightly weaker picture of the U.S. job market, with the total nonfarm payroll employment for June revised down by 27,000, from +14,000to -13,000, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

July's figures, however, were pushed higher, with payroll growth revised to 79,000 from the initially reported 73,000. 

The numbers in July caused quite a stir, with President Donald Trump firing Erika McEntarfer, head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics, after the report showed a deteriorating job market.

U.S. nonfarm payrolls rose by 22K in August, well below the +75K expected and the 79K jobs added in the prior month, which was revised up from +73K, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

Taking both the June and July's revised figures means employment in those months was 21,000 lower than previously thought.

data.title: Stock index futures rise as labor data come in cooler than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures were largely in the green on Friday as the jobs report came in cooler than expected. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.7%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.12%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 7 basis points to 3.53%.

The U.S. added far fewer jobs than expected in August; the unemployment rate rose to 4.3%.

"Today sees the first of the three massive days for markets in the next nine business days. The payrolls are today, the CPI is next Thursday, and then the FOMC decision the following Wednesday. Ahead of today's start to this run, markets have put in a strong performance over the past 24 hours, after soft data meant investors became increasingly convinced that the Fed would cut rates this month," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.  

The July nonfarm payrolls report had caused quite a stir, with President Donald Trump firing Erika McEntarfer, head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics, after the report showed a deteriorating job market.

"The August employment data is due. Falling survey response rates, rising complexity, and less funding for data collection have caused data quality to deteriorate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"A range of indicators signal a weaker labor market, without causing middle-income consumers to weaken much. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s data dependency focus means a rogue number might deter a rate cut, but markets assume the narrative of economic weakness takes priority," Donovan added.  

Wall Street traded higher on Thursday as investors weighed fresh jobs data for clues on the labor market’s strength

data.title: Canada's unemployment rate rises to 7.1% in August
data.description:
• The unemployment rate in Canada increased to 7.1% in August from 6.9% in July of 2025.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).

Currency: (USD:CAD) (USD:CAD)

data.title: U.S. adds far fewer jobs than expected in August, unemployment rate rises to 4.3%
data.description:
U.S. nonfarm payrolls rose by 22K in August, well below the +75K expected and the 79K jobs added in the prior month, which was revised up from +73K according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

The unemployment rate advanced to 4.3% from 4.2% in July, matching the consensus.

Note the annual revision of the BLS's payrolls benchmark is slated for release next week, making it the last major jobs data before the policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee gathers on Sept. 16-17.

data.title: BLS says website hit by technical issues before August jobs report
data.description:
The Bureau of Labor Statistics said Friday it's experiencing "technical difficulties" on its website just before releasing the closely watched August nonfarm payrolls report.

“Sorry, we are currently experiencing technical difficulties," the BLS said in a statement. "All BLS data retrieval tools will be available as soon as we’ve resolved the problem.”

The jobs report is slated to be published at 8:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: Elemental Altus, EMX Royalty reach merger deal to form new mid-tier royalty company
data.description:
EMX Royalty (NYSE:EMX) +2.1% pre-market Friday after Elemental Altus Royalties (OTCQX:ELEMF) agreed to acquire the company, with the merged company to continue under the new name of Elemental Royalty, creating a royalty company with combined revenue guidance of US$70M in 2025.

Under the deal terms, EMX (NYSE:EMX) shareholders will receive 0.2822 Elemental Altus (OTCQX:ELEMF) shares for each EMX share held if the consolidation is completed prior to the effective time, or 2.822 Elemental Altus shares for each EMX share if the consolidation is not completed prior to the effective time as the consideration.

In support of the transaction, Tether Investments agreed to purchase 75M Elemental Altus (OTCQX:ELEMF) shares at C$1.84 each for aggregate gross proceeds of US$100M.

Elemental Royalty would have 16 producing royalties contributing to a projected adjusted revenue of US$80M in 2026, positioning the merged company as a new mid-tier streaming and royalty company.

data.title: Hyperscaler capex helps AI boom, but some risks remain, Goldman Sach says
data.description:
AI-exposed equities continued to stay at the center of Wall Street's rally; however, there are some cracks that are hard to ignore, according to Goldman Sachs. 

AI stocks have risen an additional 17% year-to-date, following their 32% rally in 2024, and a lot of that momentum comes from heavy infrastructure behind AI, especially from companies such as Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL)(GOOG), Meta (META), and Oracle (ORCL).

The companies have significantly increased capital expenditure, pushing their combined 2025 spending forecast to $368 billion, up $100 billion since January. 

“The earnings and returns of firms involved in the build-out of this infrastructure—i.e., semiconductors, electrical equipment companies, technology hardware firms, and power suppliers—have benefited from these sizable capex investments,” Goldman Sachs said in a note. 

However, Goldman Sachs warned that the eventual slowdown in capex growth could pressure valuations.

“Consensus estimates imply that the hyperscalers will maintain their pace of capex growth in 3Q 2025, likely extending the performance of AI Phase 2 stocks. However, analysts currently assume a sharp deceleration in 4Q 2025 and 2026,” analyst Ryan Hammond said. 

The big tech names still remain expensive, but are not at bubble levels. The five largest stocks in the index Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) trade at a P/E multiple of 28x, compared with 40x at the peak in 2021 and 50x at the peak in the tech bubble, the note added, Hammond added. 

However, there are few stocks, namely Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir (PLTR), that carry much loftier valuations. 

Goldman Sachs also noted that from a productive perspective, firms still remained in the early days of the AI adoption. 

Management commentary showed that many S&P 500 firms were starting to describe specific AI use cases in their businesses, with the largest share of companies discussing customer support/call centers (24%), coding and engineering (24%), and marketing (23%), Goldman Sachs noted. 

Here are some Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (NASDAQ:AIQ), (NASDAQ:BOTZ), (NYSEARCA:DTEC), (BATS:WTAI), (NASDAQ:XAIX), (NASDAQ:WISE), (NYSEARCA:GINN), (NASDAQ:ROBT), (NYSEARCA:TECB), (NASDAQ:XT), (NYSEARCA:THNQ), and (NYSEARCA:CHAT)

Here are some S&P ETFs: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).
